events.title: Prayer Night

events.description: Join us for Chi Alpha Prayer at UC Merced! Fridays at 7 pm in GLACIER 160, we gather to seek God together through prayer, worship, and encouragement. It's a peaceful space to recharge, connect with God, and support one another. All are welcome, whether you're new to prayer or have been walking with Jesus for years.

events.title: Chi Alpha Gathering
events.subtitle: COB2 130
events.description: Join us for Chi Alpha Gatherings at UC Merced! Wednesdays at 7 pm in COB2 130, we gather for a welcoming time of community, worship, prayer, and engaging in God's Word. Whether you're exploring faith, looking for meaningful friendships, or wanting to grow deeper in your walk with Jesus, you'll find a place here. Everyone is welcome!

events.title: Agricultural Insects and Plants Monthly Meeting
events.subtitle: BSP 101
events.description: Agricultural Insects and Plants Monthly Meeting is a regular meeting where graduate and undergraduate students discuss insects and plants that are important in agriculture. Participants talk about insects that damage crops, beneficial insects such as pollinators and natural enemies, and how plants respond to insect feeding. They share research findings, field observations, and practical, environmentally responsible pest management approaches. The group also discusses potential future events, outreach activities, career paths, and related academic or professional opportunities. The overall purpose is to exchange knowledge and improve how agricultural systems are understood and managed.

events.title: ASBMB Game Night & Scrapbooking!
events.subtitle: SSB 160
events.description: Come unwind and connect with fellow members at our ASBMB Game Night & Scrapbooking event! Test your wits in a fun Jeopardy-style game, get creative with our scrapbooking project, and enjoy great conversation in a relaxed, friendly atmosphere.

events.title: Sigma Chi Province Workshop
events.subtitle: COB 105, COB 110, COB 114, COB 113, COB 263, COB 267, COB 265
events.description: Sigma Chi – California Central Valley Province Workshop. We’re looking forward to bringing brothers together for a focused and productive day of leadership development, collaboration, and best-practice sharing across the province. Brothers are invited from across the Central Valley province, and will be visiting for the day.The event will begin with all the invited chapters, coming together to go through a small workshop, and then going into breakout room intermittently. One large room is going to be reserved, as well as 7 breakout rooms.

Its about inspiring ideas of fashion, art, and self-expression as we showcase the next generation of artists making their mark.

events.title: Tuesday Tabling
events.subtitle: Scholars Lane
events.description: We will be tabling on campus to connect with the student body and share more about our organization. We invite students to stop by our table to meet our members, learn about upcoming events, and engage with us both in person and through our digital platforms.To make the experience a bit sweeter, we will be handing out Finals Hope Packets to students who come by to say hi or ask questions. Our goal is to foster a sense of community and provide a space for students to connect with one another.

events.title: Chipotle Fundraiser - Tiny Health Tales
events.subtitle: 3110 R St, STE A. Merced
events.description: 🌯 Chipotle Fundraiser for Tiny Health Tales! 🌯Join us on May 6th from 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM at Chipotle (3110 R Street, Ste A, Merced) to support Tiny Health Tales!Enjoy a delicious meal while giving back—a portion of the proceeds will go toward supporting our club’s activities and initiatives.Bring your friends, family, and appetite, and help us make a difference in our community!Thank you for your support—we hope to see you there! 💚

events.title: Our Culture, Our Community: Black Student Union Community Day
events.subtitle: Merced High School
events.description: Join the UC Merced Black Student Union, Merced High School Black Student Union, & the NAACP - Merced Branch for Our Culture, Our Community: BSU Community Day, a vibrant and intentional event dedicated to uplifting, connecting, and empowering students from historically marginalized communities across the Central Valley. This event is designed to introduce prospective high school students and their families to the opportunities, resources, and supportive networks available at UC Merced. Through interactive tabling, campus resource engagement, and community-building activities, attendees will gain insight into academic programs, student services, and pathways to higher education. Community Day centers marginalized student experiences while fostering a broader sense of belonging, cultural pride, and access for all underrepresented students. Participants will have the opportunity to connect with campus departments, student organizations, and connect students who are committed to supporting their success. Come experience a day rooted in community, culture, and connection -- we look forward to welcoming you.

events.title: Step Into Healthcare
events.subtitle: Conference Center Room 105
events.description: We will be having a CPR training event where UCM and highschool students will come together to be trained. A CPR instructor will come out and the students will receive CPR training and take their certification test at the end of the training. Following this, students will be taken to lunch and then the highschool students will receive an admissions talk after.

events.title: Aguas Frescas Fundraiser
events.subtitle: Scholars Lane
events.description: MEDLIFE is continuing our Aguas Frescas Fundraiser on Scholars Lane! Come grab a refreshing drink while supporting our Service Learning Trip (SLT), where we work to improve access to healthcare and resources in underserved communities. Your support helps make this experience possible for our members. Bring your friends, stay refreshed, and support a great cause!

events.title: Manitos Social Event
events.subtitle: GRAN 145
events.description: “The Manitos Social event is designed to strengthen the connection between mentors and mentees within our SHPE chapter by providing a relaxed and engaging environment outside of formal meetings. The purpose of this event is to build meaningful relationships, encourage open communication, and foster a sense of community among participants. Through interactive activities and group engagement, mentees will have the opportunity to seek guidance, ask questions, and learn from their mentors’ experiences, while mentors can further develop their leadership and support skills. This event also serves to improve retention and involvement within the Manitos program by creating a welcoming space where members feel comfortable, supported, and connected.

events.description: The Paleontology and Life Organization is deploying 6 cameras in natural grassland habitat to collect observational data documenting wildlife around Merced!

events.title: Clinic#1
events.subtitle: modesto sikh temple
events.description: We’re planning to host a clinic at the Modesto Sikh Temple where we’ll offer blood sugar and blood pressure checks, with a doctor on site. This will provide vital health screenings to the community, particularly those who may not otherwise get regular checks. The event will raise awareness about preventive health care and offer direct, on-the-spot guidance, contributing to overall wellness. We believe this will be a valuable service to the community
